title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye surprised a fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt at a Bruins game at TD Garden.
The Patriots quarterback attended the game after participating in a charity event raising over $4 million for Boston Children's Hospital.
Drake Maye, together with his wife Ann Michael, showed support for the fan on the Jumbotron during Bruins' 6-3 win.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers to be inducted into Basketball Hall of Fame as part of Class of 2026.
Rivers coached the Celtics to their 2008 NBA championship, achieving a 17th title for the team.
Rivers, with a 1,191-861 coaching record, joins just 11 NBA coaches with over 1,000 career wins.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots quarterback Drake Maye shaved his head at Granite Telecommunications' charity event supporting Boston Children's Hospital.
The event raised $4.15 million, with Granite donating $2,500 per participant and family matches boosting funds.
Maye's wife, Ann Michael, assisted with the head-shaving event, highlighting their ongoing philanthropic efforts.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Officer Sean Cullen charged with improper storage of a large capacity firearm and a regular firearm.
Boston police officer found with unsecured department-issued gun and taser after a domestic dispute.
Cullen suspended by Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission, placed on administrative leave by Boston police.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Kelly Ayotte and a state trooper aided in rescuing a driver from a fiery crash at Bedford Toll Plaza.
The driver crashed into the toll plaza on the Everett Turnpike and was seriously injured Tuesday before being hospitalized.
Northbound toll lanes were partially closed for investigation, while traffic was redirected off Exit 13 on Tuesday afternoon.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel discussed possible interest in Eagles' A.J. Brown at NFL owners' meetings, indicating a trade may occur post-June 1.
Vrabel emphasized developing quarterback Drake Maye's game management skills while setting championship aspirations for the Patriots.
The Patriots aim to draft the 'best player available' with the 31st pick, while considering additions at tight end and quarterback.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, charged with murdering wife in Belchertown, told 911 operator his gun 'went off'.
Police found Diane Opper with multiple gunshot wounds in her torso; she was declared dead at Baystate Wing Hospital.
Investigators found multiple guns and casings; Mark Opper held without bail, scheduled for court on April 27.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, was arraigned on 14 charges, including assault to murder and firearm possession, following a police shootout.
The incident occurred during a police chase in Brockton, when officers attempted a traffic stop near Main and Pleasant Streets.
Andrade, with a history of violent offenses, is held without bail and faces a hearing on May 1.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A crash on Route 20 in Charlton killed one driver and injured another early Tuesday morning.
The crash occurred after a pickup truck driver, who refused to stop for police, collided with an SUV.
Charlton police and Massachusetts State Police are actively investigating the incident with the DA’s office.

title: Victim identified in fatal Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Stewart, 47, from Worcester, was identified as the victim in a fatal Roxbury hit-and-run on March 20.
The incident occurred near Southampton and Bradston Streets; Stewart was pronounced dead at a nearby hospital.
Boston police have located the vehicle but made no arrests; the investigation continues.
